sql_array: CREATE TABLE json_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, json_data TEXT);
SELECT id, json.isvalid(json_data) AS is_valid FROM json_test WHERE json_data <> '{"key": "value"}' AND NOT json.isvalid(json_data);
CREATE TABLE inet_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET);
SELECT a.id, a.ip_address <> b.ip_address AS ip_mismatch FROM inet_test a JOIN inet_test b ON a.id <> b.id WHERE json.isvalid('{"ip":"'+ a.ip_address + '"}') AND a.ip_address <> inet '192.168.1.5';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM json_test GROUP BY json.isvalid(json_data) HAVING json.isvalid(ARRAY_TO_JSON(ARRAY[json_data, NULL]));
CREATE TABLE nested_test (id INT, parent_id INT, json_data TEXT);

SELECT nt1.id, json.isvalid(nt1.json_data) AS is_valid FROM nested_test nt1 WHERE nt1.id IN (SELECT nt2.id FROM nested_test nt2 WHERE nt2.parent_id IS NULL AND nt2.json_data <> '{"data": "invalid"}');
SELECT DISTINCT json.isvalid(NULL) AS is_null_valid, COUNT(*) FROM json_test WHERE json_data IS NOT NULL GROUP BY json_data <> '{}';
CREATE TABLE mixed_types (id INT, value TEXT, ip INET);
INSERT INTO mixed_types VALUES (1, '', inet '192.168.1.1'), (2, 'example', inet '192.168.0.1');
SELECT id, json.isvalid(value) AS string_valid FROM mixed_types WHERE value <> '' OR ip IS NULL;
SELECT json_data FROM json_test WHERE json.isvalid(json_data) AND json_data <> '{"key": "value"}' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;
